content:
- Completed the switchover_to task end to end.
- Verified the tracked tree no longer contains the old requester-style switchover field names, then ran the full required gates successfully: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Updated the active task file to done/pass true and ran .ralph/task_switch.sh. Next remaining closeout is staging every tracked change, committing with the required task-finished message including the gate evidence, and pushing.

- Pushed the completed task commit d656948 to origin/master after the full gate suite had already passed.
- The task file is marked done with passes=true, task_switch ran, and the docs publish hook also completed during commit.
- Final check now is just confirming the post-push worktree state before reporting completion.

- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` plus the previous progress log.
- Confirmed the active backlog snapshot currently contains a single active candidate task: `.ralph/tasks/story-cluster-startup-friction-improvements/task-smooth-the-local-docker-cluster-startup-experience.md`.
- Opened that task file and verified it has `<passes>false</passes>`, `<priority>low</priority>`, and no `<blocked_by>` tags, so it is the highest-precedence selectable task under the bug-first and blocker rules.
- Next immediate step was to write that exact task path into `.ralph/current_task.txt` and quit.

- Read the prior progress log and the new current Ralph task, then inspected the active task file.
- Reviewed the current Docker cluster workflow across docker-compose.cluster.yml, .env.docker.example, tools/docker/common.sh, tools/docker/smoke-cluster.sh, Makefile cluster targets, README.md, and docs/src/tutorial/first-ha-cluster.md.
- Wrote a detailed first-pass implementation plan into the active task file covering a new persistent operator-facing cluster lifecycle script, shared helper refactors, make target integration, docs updates, and the required gate verification plan. Marked the end of the task file with TO BE VERIFIED and stopped before any execution work. Next turn should begin with a deep skeptical review of this plan, change at least one thing in it, and only move to NOW EXECUTE once the revised plan is solid.

- Read the active task file and found it already at TO BE VERIFIED, so this turn became the skeptical review pass rather than fresh planning.
- Re-checked the plan against Makefile, tools/docker/common.sh, tools/docker/smoke-cluster.sh, README.md, docs/src/tutorial/first-ha-cluster.md, and .env.docker.example.
- Altered the plan materially: removed the assumption that .env.docker.example should be the default operator contract, aligned the future execution with the existing Makefile/.env.docker and DOCKER_CLUSTER_PROJECT contract, biased the design away from persisted state files, added a required make status target, and added explicit verification for env-file overrides. Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE.
- Found ignored-error patterns in the Docker helper flow and created .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-docker-helper-ignored-errors.md to capture that follow-up debt instead of letting it disappear.
- Next turn should execute the approved plan directly without further exploratory work.

- Confirmed the active task file is already at NOW EXECUTE, so this turn is the direct implementation pass.
- Loaded the task content and current git status; there are pre-existing Ralph bookkeeping changes plus the earlier task file/progress edits.
- Next I am reading only the execution-plan files (Makefile, Docker helpers/scripts, README, tutorial, env example) and then I will patch the operator-facing cluster lifecycle flow.

- Read the main execution-plan files and confirmed the current contracts: Makefile still uses raw docker compose for cluster up/down, common.sh has reusable wait helpers but not operator-facing summary/status helpers, smoke-cluster duplicates env parsing and role checks, and README/tutorial docs still teach raw compose usage.
- Next I am patching the shared Docker helper layer plus a new persistent cluster lifecycle script, then I will update the Makefile/docs to make that flow the canonical operator path.

- Patched common.sh with env parsing, cluster endpoint helpers, HA summary printing, service-running checks, and a shared replication-role wait.
- Added tools/docker/cluster.sh with up/status/down subcommands, stable project/env contracts, first-run build notice, readiness checks, and printed endpoint/topology summary.
- Updated smoke-cluster to consume the shared helpers, rewired make docker cluster targets, and updated README/tutorial/how-to docs to point to the new statusable lifecycle flow.
- Next I am doing shell verification plus live up/status/down checks, then the required full gate suite.

- The first live cluster up reached running containers and then failed in the final summary print because ha_state_field was invoking Python incorrectly against JSON containing lowercase booleans.
- Patched that helper immediately; next step is to re-run status against the already-started stack, then continue with down and the explicit env-file override verification.

- Default-contract live verification succeeded after one helper fix: status printed the expected compose/env metadata, leader, and per-node endpoints/roles against the running cluster.
- The first explicit .env.docker.example override attempt raced the default teardown and failed on published port reuse, so I am cleaning the partial override project and then re-running that override path cleanly.

- Live verification is now complete for both contracts: `tools/docker/cluster.sh up/status/down` worked with the default `.env.docker` resolution and with an explicit `.env.docker.example` override plus custom project name.
- I polished the summary output so structured HA decisions render as compact JSON strings instead of Python reprs.
- Next step is the required gate suite in order: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.

- Completed the implementation and verification pass for the local Docker cluster operator workflow.
- Required gates all passed cleanly in this turn: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint. Live manual verification also passed for tools/docker/cluster.sh up/status/down using both the default .env.docker contract and an explicit .env.docker.example override.
- Updated the task file to completed/passes true and am now running Ralph task switch, then I will stage all changes, commit with the required task-finished message including gate evidence, and push.
